concerns.text: Task T023 allows a fallback to 'standard stratified' CV if phylogenetic logic is unavailable. The Plan (Complexity Tracking) and Spec (FR-005, US-2) explicitly mandate Phylogenetically-Blocked CV or PGLS to prevent data leakage from clonal structure. No task exists to implement the mandatory phylogenetic blocking logic, leaving the core validity requirement unaddressed.

Task T030 instructs to 'assert p < 0.05 for significance'. The Spec (FR-005, SC-002) requires *measuring* and *reporting* the p-value to determine significance, not enforcing it as a hard pass/fail gate that crashes the pipeline. The task description contradicts the spec's requirement for reporting results regardless of the outcome.

Task T034 covers regenerating figures/tables. Spec US-3 (Acceptance Scenario 3) and SC-004 require the *entire pipeline* (ingestion through modeling) to be reproducible end-to-end. The task scope is too narrow, missing the requirement to re-execute data ingestion and feature extraction steps to verify the 'Single Source of Truth' principle.

Task T029 implements PGLS residual permutation. The Plan (Technical Context) and Spec (FR-005) require a phylogenetic tree as input for this test. No task exists in Phase 3 (Data Ingestion/Processing) to explicitly generate or infer the phylogenetic tree from the SNP data required by T029. The dependency is missing.

Task T017 handles the edge case of <50 isolates per class. However, the Spec (Edge Cases) also defines a critical failure mode: 'What happens when all classes have <50 isolates? The system must abort...'. No task explicitly implements the global check and abort logic (Error E004) required by the spec.
